players.name: Jordan Pickford
players.bio: England's first-choice keeper and penalty shootout hero of 2018. The Everton shot-stopper brings vocal leadership and sharp reflexes. Washington's finest export, now a seasoned 84-cap veteran at his second World Cup.
players.facts: Saved Colombia's Carlos Bacca in the 2018 shootout — England's first WC penalty shootout win. The gloves delivered!

players.name: Ezri Konsa
players.bio: A composed, athletic centre-back who rose through Charlton's academy before finding his home at Aston Villa. Strong in the air and comfortable on the ball, Konsa has become a reliable defensive pillar for the Three Lions.
players.facts: Born in Newham, he represented England despite Angolan heritage — and has a goal to his name internationally. Defenders who score are a rare breed.

players.name: Nico O'Reilly
players.bio: Born in 2005, Nico O'Reilly is one of the youngest players at this tournament. The Manchester City midfielder offers energy and technical quality, making his World Cup debut at just 21. A generational talent in the making.
players.facts: O'Reilly was born the same year England last hosted a major final. He's the future — and the future arrived very quickly.

players.name: Declan Rice
players.bio: England's engine room. Declan Rice evolved from a commanding defensive midfielder into a box-to-box force at Arsenal. With 73 caps and 7 international goals, he's the heartbeat of this squad — and rarely gives the ball away.
players.facts: Rice was released by Chelsea's academy at 14. He's since become England's most important midfielder. Chelsea fans, look away.

players.name: John Stones
players.bio: A Barnsley lad who became a ball-playing defender of the highest order. Stones has won multiple Premier League titles with Man City and brings calmness and elegance to England's backline at his second World Cup.
players.facts: Stones once played as a midfielder in youth football. Given how often he dribbles out from the back, some days it still shows.

players.name: Marc Guéhi
players.bio: Born in Abidjan but raised in England, Guehi chose the Three Lions and hasn't looked back. A commanding, left-footed centre-back who reads the game brilliantly. Now at Manchester City, he's thriving at the highest level.
players.facts: Guehi wore the armband for England despite being just 23 at Euro 2024. Cool head, big moments — he's been doing it for years.

players.name: Bukayo Saka
players.bio: England's golden boy and Arsenal's talisman. Saka's directness, creativity and relentless work rate make him almost unplayable on his day. With 14 international goals at just 24, his World Cup ceiling is sky-high.
players.facts: Saka missed the crucial penalty at Euro 2021, then returned to Euro 2024 as one of England's best players. Character defined.

players.name: Elliot Anderson
players.bio: A dynamic central midfielder from Whitley Bay with an eye for goal and a tireless engine. Anderson broke through at Nottingham Forest and quickly earned international recognition. Fearless and technically gifted for his age.
players.facts: Anderson was born in Whitley Bay — the same town as legendary England keeper Gordon Banks was raised. Coincidence? Probably, yes.

players.name: Harry Kane
players.bio: England's all-time record goalscorer with 79 goals in 114 caps. Kane is a generational striker — brilliant in the air, lethal from range, and a masterful link-up player. Still hunting that elusive major trophy at 32.
players.kit: Nike Phantom GT Elite Dynamic Fit FG
players.facts: Kane has scored more England goals than Gary Lineker, Wayne Rooney, and Bobby Charlton. He did it with a free-kick, naturally.

players.name: Jude Bellingham
players.bio: Real Madrid's teenage sensation turned senior star. Bellingham is audacious, technically brilliant, and has a habit of scoring in crucial moments. At 22, he's already one of the world's best — England built their dreams around him.
players.facts: Bellingham wore the No.22 shirt at Birmingham City before the world knew his name. Now he wears No.10 for England. Upgrade complete.

players.name: Marcus Rashford
players.bio: A mercurial left winger with electric pace and a powerful shot, Rashford has reinvented himself at Barcelona after a difficult spell at United. With 18 international goals, he brings big-game experience and undeniable flair.
players.kit: Nike Mercurial Superfly VII Elite FG
players.facts: Rashford made his Man United debut at 18 and scored twice. He's now at Barcelona. Life comes at you fast, Marcus.

players.name: Tino Livramento
players.bio: An energetic, attack-minded right-back from Croydon who excels in one-on-one situations. Livramento has impressed for Newcastle with his pace and composure. Still making his name internationally, but the ceiling is enormous.
players.facts: Livramento was a Chelsea academy product who left for Southampton, then Newcastle — and ended up at the World Cup. Patience pays.

players.name: Dean Henderson
players.bio: A commanding, vocal keeper from Cumbria who eventually found his footing at Crystal Palace after years of loan moves. Henderson is a reliable backup who commands his area well and pushes Pickford hard in training.
players.facts: Dean Henderson went on seven loan spells before establishing himself as a top-flight starter. Persistence is its own superpower.

players.name: Jordan Henderson
players.bio: A true stalwart of English football. Jordan Henderson captained Liverpool to the Premier League and Champions League, accumulating 90 caps. Now at Brentford, the veteran leader brings experience and drive to the squad.
players.facts: Henderson lifted the Premier League trophy in 2020, ending Liverpool's 30-year wait. He then did a lap of honour in the rain. Iconic.

players.name: Dan Burn
players.bio: At 6ft 7in, Dan Burn is hard to miss. The Blyth-born left-back is a dependable, no-nonsense defender who has reinvented himself at Newcastle. A late bloomer on the international stage, but fiercely committed when called upon.
players.facts: Burn is one of the tallest outfield players at the tournament. He was also a Newcastle fan as a kid. Living the dream, literally.

players.name: Kobbie Mainoo
players.bio: The teenage metronome from Stockport who arrived at Man United and immediately looked like he'd been playing Premier League football for years. Mainoo's composure and technical ability belies his age — a star in the making.
players.facts: Mainoo scored in the 2024 FA Cup Final for Man United aged just 19. Stockport's most famous footballing export since... well, ever.

players.name: Morgan Rogers
players.bio: A versatile and creative left winger who thrives in tight spaces and loves to drive at defenders. Rogers caught the eye at Aston Villa with his clever movement and ambition. His World Cup call-up was earned, not given.
players.facts: Rogers was born in Halesowen and came through Man City's academy — returning to haunt them for Villa. The Midlands produces quietly.

players.name: Anthony Gordon
players.bio: Liverpudlian by birth, Geordie by adoption. Gordon's energy, directness and goal threat on the left flank have made him a fan favourite at Newcastle and an international regular. Works tirelessly and causes defenders real headaches.
players.facts: Gordon started at Everton's academy before joining Newcastle — a transfer that didn't go down well on Merseyside. At all.

players.name: Ollie Watkins
players.bio: The man who scored THAT goal against the Netherlands at Euro 2024 to send England to the final. Watkins is a relentless, intelligent striker who brings selfless pressing and clinical finishing. Torquay to the World Cup — quite the journey.
players.facts: Watkins came off the bench at Euro 2024 and scored a last-minute winner. He had been on the pitch approximately 96 seconds.

players.name: Noni Madueke
players.bio: A dynamic and direct right winger who grew up in north London before thriving in European football. Madueke's ability to beat his man and cut inside onto his left foot makes him a dangerous option off the bench or from the start.
players.facts: Madueke spent time at PSV in the Netherlands before returning to England via Arsenal. He took the long route and it worked perfectly.

players.name: Eberechi Eze
players.bio: Silky, inventive and always watchable. Eze's footwork and vision can unlock any defence, and his move to Arsenal has elevated him to another level. The London-born playmaker is a joy to watch — and opponents dread facing him.
players.facts: Eze suffered a serious Achilles injury in 2024 and came back better than ever. Obstacles, apparently, are just suggestions to him.

players.name: Ivan Toney
players.bio: A powerful, confident striker who makes defenders' lives extremely uncomfortable. Toney served a betting ban before returning to club football and heading to Saudi Arabia. His international record shows he delivers when given the chance.
players.facts: Toney waited over a year between England appearances due to his FA betting ban. He returned and scored on his comeback. Of course he did.

players.name: James Trafford
players.bio: A towering, shot-stopping goalkeeper from the tiny Cumbrian village of Greysouthen. Trafford came through the Manchester City academy and has quickly established himself as England's most exciting young keeper. A future No.1.
players.facts: Greysouthen has a population of around 300. James Trafford might be the most famous person ever to come from it. By some distance.

players.name: Reece James
players.bio: A marauding right-back with the technical quality of a winger and the determination of a warrior. James has been plagued by injuries but remains one of Europe's finest fullbacks when fit. Chelsea's captain with world-class potential.
players.facts: Reece James is the brother of England's Lauren James. The James family household must have quite the trophy cabinet.

players.name: Djed Spence
players.bio: An athletic and attack-minded right-back who can operate as a wing-back with devastating effect. Spence's raw pace and energy make him a potent weapon. He's worked hard to establish himself at Spurs after various loan adventures.
players.facts: Spence had loan spells at five clubs before making it at Spurs. He travelled the long road — and arrived at a World Cup.

players.name: Jarell Quansah
players.bio: A composed, intelligent centre-back who came through Liverpool's academy and moved to Bayer Leverkusen to continue his development. Quansah is a commanding presence in the air and comfortable with the ball — a rising defensive talent.
players.facts: Quansah moved to Leverkusen — the club that went unbeaten all season in 2023-24. He clearly has good taste in employers.
